¶2842.content: # A.3.3 Handling of information received from trackside

¶2843.content: - A.3.3.1 Before it  can  be  accepted  and  used  by  the  ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment, raw information received from trackside is subject to various checks, which can lead to the individual  rejection/ignoring  of  information  (e.g.  Movement  Authority  not  valid  for  the train  orientation),  the  ignoring  of  a  whole  balise  telegram  or  the  rejection  of  a  whole message. The SRS clauses/sections corresponding to these checks are gathered in Table 17.

¶2845.content: # ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

¶2847.content: Table 17: Check of raw data received from trackside

¶2848.content: - A.3.3.2 With the exception of the clauses/sections referred to in  Table 17 and of the clause 3.16.3.5.1, all the clauses in this specification in which trackside information is referred to  (through  the  terms  'balise',  'telegram',  'balise  group  [message]',  'message', '[name of] information'...), shall be applied assuming that the information has not been ignored/rejected by the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment due to all the checks listed in Table 17.

¶2849.content: - A.3.3.3 Example  1:  clause  3.16.2.4.9  has  to  be  understood  as  follows:  'If  a <consistent> message <composed with telegrams that have passed the system version check, that have  not  been  ignored  because  of  a  VBC,  that  have  not  been  ignored  because  of duplication, and that have been received while no reverse movement was performed> has been received < with one of its telegrams composing it> containing the information 'default balise information' <which is valid for the train orientation (or the balise group crossing  direction  for  NL  or  SL  engines)  and  which  has  passed  the  level  and  mode filters> , the driver shall be informed.'

¶2850.content: - A.3.3.4 Example 2: as a result of the clause A.3.3.2, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment will  not  apply requirements in relation to the content of the telegram or the message such as:

¶2851.content: # ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

¶2852.content: -  clause  3.6.2.2.2  a),  in  case  the  balise  group  message  is  rejected  because  not consistent (e.g. according to 3.16.2.4.1), it does not become LRBG

¶2853.content: -  clause  3.16.3.3.3.1,  in  case  the  radio  message  is  rejected  because  of  its  time stamp,  this  latter  will  not  be  used  by  the  on-board  to  check  the  supervision  of sequence of further messages

¶2854.content: -  clause  3.17.2.3,  in  case  the  balise  telegram  is  ignored  because  of  its  system version number (e.g. according to 3.17.3.5 d)), this latter will not affect the system version operated by the on-board

¶2855.content: -  clause 3.18.2.5 2 nd  bullet, in case a message from a balise group marked as linked is rejected because  the balise group was  not announced  by  linking (see 3.16.2.4.3), the balise group country or region identifier will not be compared with the one(s) of the currently applicable set of National Values

¶2856.content: # A.3.4 Handling of Accepted and Stored Information in specific Situations

¶2858.content: - A.3.4.1.1 All data that can be stored onboard after being accepted may be influenced in special situations.

¶2859.content: - A.3.4.1.2 The situations acting on the 'status' of stored information are:

¶2860.content: - the execution of a conditional emergency stop (3.10.2.2);

¶2861.content: - the reception of a shortened MA (3.8.5.1.3, 3.8.5.1.4);

¶2862.content: -  the stored MA is shortened due to a section time-out (3.8.4.2.2);

¶2863.content: - the  SvL  is  shifted  (to  the  DP  if  any  or  to  the  EOA)  due  to  an  overlap  time-out (3.8.4.4.2) ;

¶2864.content: - the stored MA is shortened due to an end section time-out (3.8.4.1.2);

¶2865.content: -  a cooperative MA revocation is granted by the onboard (3.8.6.2);

¶2866.content: - inconsistency  in  a  balise  group  marked  as  unlinked  and  the  train  is  at  standstill (3.16.2.5.2);

¶2867.content: - a linking reaction led to a service brake and the train is at standstill (3.16.2.6.2) ;

¶2868.content: - the  reaction  due  to  the  supervision  of  the  safe  radio  connection  led  to  a  service brake and the train is at standstill (3.16.3.4.5 b) ;

¶2869.content: -  the train category, axle load category, loading gauge or traction system is changed and the train is at standstill (3.18.3.7) ;

¶2870.content: -  driver closes the desk during SoM ;

¶2871.content: - RAMS  related  supervision  functions  led  to  a  service  brake  and  the  train  is  at standstill (3.16.2.7)

¶2872.content: # ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

¶2873.content: -  inconsistency  in  a  balise  group  marked  as  linked  and  no  linking  is  used  onboard and the train is at standstill (3.16.2.4.4.2)

¶2874.content: - the Limit of Authority becomes an End of Authority and the on-board considers an SvL (3.8.4.3.2)

¶2875.content: - A.3.4.1.3 Depending on the situation, the action shall be one of the following:

¶2877.content: - data is reset (set to initial states)

¶2880.content: D = Deleted U = Unchanged R = Reset TBR = To Be Revalidated

¶2885.content: - [2]:  The  considered  situations  cannot  occur  when  a  list  of  balises  to  be  used  in  SR  is  available  onboard. Indeed, the onboard is in SR mode and since no MA or track description are stored onboard, no new SvL may be defined.

¶2886.content: - [3]: In case of reception of a new non-infill MA (situation b or f), the stored MA is fully replaced with the new one. In case of reception of a new infill MA (situation b), the stored MA is replaced beyond the infill location reference, i.e. the balise group at the next main signal

¶2887.content: - [4]: The considered situations a-d, f, h, i cannot occur when the default gradient for a TSR is used on-board.

¶2888.content: - [5]: The considered situation cannot occur because acceptance of this information has led to exit from SoM procedure.

¶2889.content: - [6]: The considered situations b-d, f cannot occur when a TAF request is stored on-board

¶2890.content: - [7]: If the start location of the Mode Profile is beyond the new SvL, the acknowledgement window of the Mode Profile shall be deleted as well

¶2891.content: - [8]:  only  if  the  location  where  to  start  to  display  the  text  is  beyond  the  new  SvL;  otherwise  all  the  text information (i.e. including end location where to stop display, if any) shall remain unchanged

¶2892.content: - [9]: unchanged if the onboard is in SH mode

¶2893.content: - [10]: beyond the current max safe front end position of the train

¶2894.content: - [11]:  the  ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall consider the current estimated front end and max safe front end positions of the train, as the EOA and SvL respectively, with no release speed

¶2895.content: - [12]: If the start location of the Mode Profile is beyond the current max safe front end, the acknowledgement window of the Mode Profile shall be deleted as well

¶2896.content: - [13]: only if the location where to start to display the text is beyond the current max safe front end; otherwise all the text information (i.e. including end location where to stop display, if any) shall remain unchanged

¶2897.content: - [14]: In case of reception of a new non-infill MA with or without Mode Profile (situation b or f), the stored Mode Profile is deleted. In case of reception of a new infill MA (situation b), the stored Mode Profile is deleted only beyond the infill location reference, i.e. the balise group at the next main signal

¶2927.content: # A.3.5 Handling of Actions in Specific Situations

¶2928.content: - A.3.5.1 Regards actions executed in reference to location information received from trackside, the  on-board  equipment  shall  ensure  that  the  action  related  to  a  location  is  neither reverted,  nor  executed  twice.  Situations  to  be  considered  shall  include  reverse movement  (initiated  by  driver  or  due  to  roll-away)  and  the  sub-sequent  forward movement,  or  adjustment  of  train  position  on  passing  a  new  LRBG.  This  rule  shall apply to the following actions:

¶2930.content: -  Request to acknowledge new level, level transition (see SRS chapter 5.10)

¶2931.content: -  Start and stop displaying plain or fixed text messages (see 3.12.3)

¶2932.content: -  Request to acknowledge a mode profile, mode transition due to mode profile (see 3.12.4)

¶2933.content: -  Start and stop accepting radio infill information (see 3.9.3)

¶2935.content: -  Actions related to track condition information (see 3.12.1) with the exception of big metal masses and non stopping areas

¶2936.content: -  Permission to initiate Reversing mode, i.e., limits of Reversing Area (see 3.15.4)

¶2937.content: -  Start and stop Track Ahead free request to driver (see 3.15.5)

¶2938.content: -  Start and stop calculation of geographical position (see 3.6.6)

¶2939.content: -  Substitute the supervision of the LX start location as the EOA/SvL by the inclusion of the LX speed restriction in the MRSP (see 5.16.2 and 5.16.3)

¶2940.content: - A.3.5.2 Once  the  ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment has received  a  balise  group  message (i.e.  once  it  has  received  the  last  balise  telegram  of  the  balise  group),  the  action(s) resulting from its content shall take into account the train position measured at the time of reception of this last telegram and shall take precedence on any other action related to a further location that is reached before the message has been fully processed.

¶2941.content: - A.3.5.2.1 Example 1: in level 1, the crossing of the EOA/LOA location with the min safe antenna, before a new extended MA (received when the min safe antenna was in rear of the EOA/LOA)  has  been  processed,  will  not  lead  to  train  trip.  In  other  terms  the replacement of the EOA/LOA is considered by the on-board as happening before the min safe antenna crosses the EOA/LOA  location (i.e. preventing that clause 3.13.10.2.7 applies).

¶2942.content: - A.3.5.2.2 Example 2: when the override function is active, the crossing of the former EOA/LOA location by the min safe antenna, before a 'Stop if in SR' information (received when the min safe antenna was in rear of the former EOA/LOA) has been processed, will not

¶2943.content: lead to the end of the override procedure followed by a train trip due to 'Stop if in SR'. In  other  terms,  both  the  deletion  of  the  former  EOA/LOA  and  the  end  of  override procedure (see 5.8.3.1.3 and 5.8.4.1 c)) are considered by the on-board as simultaneously happening before the min safe antenna crosses the former EOA/LOA location.

¶2944.content: # A.3.6 Deletion of accepted and stored information when used

¶2946.content: - A.3.6.1.1 When the train moves in the direction of its train orientation, storage capacity occupied by  trackside  information  no  longer  used,  i.e.,  the  related  on-board  functionality  has been completed, shall be made available immediately.

¶2947.content: - A.3.6.1.1.1 Note:  The  requirement  is  needed  to  allow  trackside  to  predict  the  storage  capacity available  on-board  in  order  to  comply  with  dimensioning  rules  regards  information stored on-board given in Subset 040.

¶2949.content: - A.3.6.2.1 Following information shall remain stored on-board for a distance defined by a fixed value in rear of the min safe rear end position of the train:

¶2950.content: -  location dependent static speed restrictions , i.e., SSP, ASP, TSR, LX SR, PBD SR (see 3.11.2.2)

¶2952.content: -  reduced adhesion information received from trackside,

¶2954.content: - A.3.6.2.1.1 Note: The above information remains stored for the case of a reverse movement:

¶2955.content: -  With  the  exception  of  the  track  condition  'Big  metal  masses',    the  stored information  allows  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  to  calculate  speed supervision limits after a reverse movement (roll-away, or initiated by the driver)

¶2956.content: -  Track condition 'Big metal masses' is needed also for a reverse movement itself to avoid any false alarms due to Big metal masses in the track.

¶2957.content: - A.3.6.2.1.2 Note: The distance to intervention of the roll away or reverse movement supervision is determined by a National/Default value.  This is also true for a reverse movement in Post trip mode. However, following an intervention, the train will not stop immediately. In order to keep the on-board functionality simple, a fixed distance value was chosen to define an unambiguous location in rear of the train where the above information is no longer required and the related on-board storage capacity is made available again.

¶2958.content: # ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

¶2959.content: # A.3.7 Calculation of the basic deceleration

¶2960.content: - A.3.7.1 The brake percentage (λ) shall be converted into two different input parameters:

¶2961.content: λo = λ for calculation of emergency brake deceleration (A_brake_emergency(V))

¶2962.content: λo = MIN (λ, 135) for calculation of service brake deceleration (A_brake_service(V))

¶2963.content: where λ is the brake percentage defined as part of Train Data.

¶2964.content: - A.3.7.2 The calculation of the basic deceleration (A_basic(V)) shall use a common algorithm that will be used twice, once for the service brake and once for the emergency brake.

¶2965.content: - A.3.7.3 The speed limit for the first step shall be calculated as V_lim = x * λo y .

¶2966.content: V_lim is the speed limit for the first step in km/h

¶2969.content: - A.3.7.4 The first step of the basic deceleration shall be calculated as AD_0 = A * λo + B

¶2970.content: AD_0 is the basic deceleration in m/s 2  for 0 ≤ speed ≤ V_lim.

¶2973.content: - A.3.7.5 The following steps of the basic deceleration shall be calculated by means of a set of polynomials of the third order with the following format:

¶2975.content: and with the following values for n (all speed limits in km/h):

¶2976.content:
| n = 1   | valid for V_lim < speed ≤ 100 to be ignored                 | if V_lim < 100 if V_lim ≥ 100       |
|---------|-------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|
| n = 2   | valid for V_lim < speed ≤ 120 valid for 100 < speed ≤ 120   | if 100 < V_lim < 120 if V_lim ≤ 100 |
| n = 3   | valid for V_lim < speed ≤ 150 valid for 120 < speed ≤ 150   | if 120 < V_lim < 150 if V_lim ≤ 120 |
| n = 4   | valid for V_lim < speed ≤ 180 valid for 150 < speed ≤ 180   | if 150 < V_lim < 180 if V_lim ≤ 150 |
| n = 5   | to be ignored valid for V_lim < speed valid for 180 < speed | if V_lim ≥ 180 if V_lim > 180       |
|         |                                                             | if V_lim ≤ 180                      |

If justified by the specific brake system of the train other values of these coefficients, which lead to shorter values of the equivalent brake build up time for the service brake, may be used.

¶3068.content: - A.3.10.1 The purpose of service brake feedback is to reduce the distance between the SBI and EBI supervision limits and between the SBI and SBD curves.

¶3069.content: - A.3.10.2 The on-board shall consider the service brake feedback as available for use if:

¶3070.content: - The service brake feedback is implemented, AND

¶3071.content: - The national value does not inhibit its use.

¶3072.content: # ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

¶3073.content: - A.3.10.3 Two different types of feedback from the service brake are specified, main brake pipe pressure and brake cylinder pressure. The algorithms below are made for main brake pipe pressure. When brake cylinder pressure is used instead this shall be converted into a fictive main brake pressure value in the following way:

¶3074.content: p = fictive main brake pipe pressure (kPa)

¶3076.content: k1 = vehicle dependent constant (set by engineering of ETCS on-board; k1 is normally between 2.0 and 2.7)

¶3078.content: - A.3.10.4 The value of T_bs1 and T_bs2 shall be calculated according to the following algorithm to take the service brake feedback into account:

¶3079.content: p = current main brake pipe pressure (or fictive main brake pipe pressure calculated in A.3.10.3)

¶3080.content: p0 = reference pressure when not braking

¶3081.content: p1 = pressure at which the train starts to brake = p0 - 30

¶3082.content: p2 = pressure limit, under which T_bs1 and T_bs2 are locked = p0 - 60

¶3083.content: p3 = pressure at full service brake = p0 - 150

¶3084.content: Q_feedback_active = a Boolean stating whether the feedback function is active, i.e. once it  has  started  to  reduce  T_bs1  and  T_bs2  until  the  ceiling  speed  monitoring  is entered.

¶3085.content: Q_Tbslocked = a boolean stating whether T_bs1 and T_bs2 have been locked to the following values due to enough main brake pipe pressure reduction:

¶3088.content: Q_displaylocked_P  =  a  boolean  stating  whether  the  displayed  permitted  speed  is locked due to SB feedback.

¶3089.content: Q_displaylocked_SBI = a boolean stating whether the displayed SBI speed (if any) is locked due to SB feedback.

¶3090.content: Q_displaylocked_TD  =  a  boolean  stating  whether  the  displayed  target  distance  is locked due to SB feedback.

¶3091.content: A displayed value is locked from the moment the SB feedback has started to reduce T_bs1  and  T_bs2  until  the  calculated  value  becomes  less  than  the  displayed  and locked value. Note: It is only SB feedback that can start the locking of the displayed values.  Once  started  and  still  locked  it  remains  locked  also  in  case  the  calculated values are increased due to other reasons (e.g. due to relocation), which will prolong the locking period.

¶3092.content: Initial values when the target speed monitoring is entered or when the release speed monitoring is entered not from target speed monitoring:

¶3094.content: # ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

¶3095.content:
```unknown
Q_feedback_active = false Q_displaylocked_P = false Q_displaylocked_SBI = false Q_displaylocked_TD = false Q_Tbslocked =false If on-board is in target speed monitoring or release speed monitoring then If Q_Tbslocked then T_bs1 = T_bs1_locked T_bs2 = T_bs2_locked Else If p > p2 then If Q_feedback_active or p ≤ p1 then Q_feedback_active = true T_bs_feedback = T_bs * (p - p3) / (p0 - p3) T_bs1 = T_bs2 = T_bs_feedback If T_bs_feedback > T_bs then T_bs1 = T_bs2 = T_bs Else if T_bs_feedback < T_bs2_locked then T_bs2 = T_bs2_locked End If Else T_bs1 = T_bs T_bs2 = T_bs End If Else T_bs1 = T_bs1_locked T_bs2 = T_bs2_locked Q_feedback_active = true Q_Tbslocked = true End If End If Else T_bs1 = T_bs T_bs2 = T_bs End if If Q_feedback_active and T_bs1 < T_bs1_prev then
```

¶3117.content: The reference pressure p0 (nominal value 500 kPa) shall be set on starting the ETCS:

¶3118.content: - To the first stable p value between 400-550 kPa achieved.

¶3119.content: - Stable in this instance means that the pressure has not varied more than ± 20 kPa over 3 seconds.

¶3120.content: The reference pressure p0 shall thereafter be adapted to the current pressure according to the following table (which applies if the calculation is performed once per second):

¶3121.content:
|    | CONDITIONS:     | ACTION:       | REMARKS             |
|----|-----------------|---------------|---------------------|
| a) | p = p0          | No change     | Constant pressure   |
| b) | p > p0          | p0 = p0 + 1,5 | Increasing pressure |
| c) | p < p0 - 30     | No change     | Braking             |
| d) | p0 > p > p0- 30 | p0 = p0  0,5 | Decreasing pressure |

¶3125.content: - A.3.10.5 Note: If T_bs1 and T_bs2 have been locked to 0s and 2 s on approaching a non zero target, the locking will remain even if the train speed comes below the target speed. This  avoids  'jumping'  indications  related  to  the  values  of  T_bs1  and  T_bs2.  It  also makes it possible to release the brakes before a speed reduction, without having the curves moving back again. It might though result in emergency brake intervention if the driver releases the brakes too early. But since EBI is not moved, this is not a safety issue. To keep 2 s between the SBI and EBI enables the service brake to be activated first and thus may avoid emergency brake.

¶3126.content: - A.3.10.6 Note: If feedback is active but T_bs1 and T_bs2 are not locked, the feedback function will  remain active until the ceiling speed monitoring is entered. This avoids 'jumping' indications in some rare situations.

¶3147.content: # ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

¶3150.content: # ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

¶3154.content: - 4.3.1.1 This  document  defines  the  modes  of  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  (see chapter 4.4 'Definition of the modes' and chapter 4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3155.content: - 4.3.1.2 This  document  gives  all  transitions  between  modes  (see  chapter  4.6  'Transitions between modes').

¶3156.content: - 4.3.1.3 This document describes the possible exchanged information between the driver and the  ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment, respect  to  the  mode  (see  chapter  4.7  'DMI depending on modes').

¶3157.content: - 4.3.1.4 This  document  describes  how the received information is filtered,  respect  to  several criteria  such  as  the  level,  the  mode,  etc..  (see  chapter  4.8  'Acceptance  of  received information').

¶3158.content: - 4.3.1.5 This  document  describes  how  the  stored  information  is  handled,  respect  to  several criteria such as the level, the mode, etc. (see chapter 4.9 'What happens to accepted and stored information when entering a given level', and chapter 4.10 'What happens to accepted and stored information when entering a given mode').

¶3159.content: - 4.3.1.6 All  the  tables  that  are  included  in  this  document  shall  be  considered  as  mandatory requirements.

¶3160.content: - 4.3.1.7 Some  notes  appear  in  this  document.  These  notes  are  here  to  help  the  reader  to understand the specifications, or to explain the reason(s) of a requirement.

¶3163.content:
| Full Supervision    | (FS)   |
|---------------------|--------|
| Limited Supervision | (LS)   |
| On Sight            | (OS)   |
| Staff Responsible   | (SR)   |
| Shunting            | (SH)   |
| Unfitted            | (UN)   |
| Passive Shunting    | (PS)   |
| Sleeping            | (SL)   |
| Stand By            | (SB)   |
| Trip                | (TR)   |

¶3164.content: # ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

¶3165.content:
| Post Trip       | (PT)   |
|-----------------|--------|
| System Failure  | (SF)   |
| Isolation       | (IS)   |
| No Power        | (NP)   |
| Non Leading     | (NL)   |
| National System | (SN)   |
| Reversing       | (RV)   |

¶3168.content: - 4.4.1.1 For each mode the following information is given:

¶3169.content: - The context of utilisation of the mode and the functions that characterise the mode (chapter 'Description').

¶3170.content: - The  ERTMS/ETCS  levels  in  which  the  mode  can  be  used  (chapter  'Used  in levels').

¶3171.content: - The  related  responsibility  of  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  and  of  the driver, once the equipment is in this mode (chapter 'Responsibilities').

¶3172.content: - 4.4.1.2 A  complete  list  of  transitions  to  and  from  each  mode  is  given  in  the  section  4.6.2 'Transitions Table' ).

¶3174.content: - 4.4.2.1 When the desk is open, a clear indication of the ERTMS/ETCS mode shall be shown to the driver.

¶3178.content: - 4.4.3.1.1 In Isolation mode, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall be physically isolated from  the  brakes  and  can  be  isolated  from  other  on-board  equipments/systems depending on the specific on-board implementation.

¶3179.content: - 4.4.3.1.2 There  shall  be  a  clear  indication  to  the  driver  that  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board equipment is isolated.

¶3180.content: - 4.4.3.1.3 To leave Isolation mode, a special operating procedure is needed (no transition from Isolation is specified). This procedure shall ensure that the on-board equipment is only put back into service when it has been proven that this is safe for operation.

¶3181.content: - 4.4.3.1.4 Note: for the list  of  main  functions  related  to this mode, refer to chapter 4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3183.content: - 4.4.3.2.1 Used in all levels: Level 0, level 1, level 2, level 3 and level NTC.

¶3185.content: - 4.4.3.3.1 Isolation of the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment is performed by the driver under his complete responsibility.

¶3186.content: - 4.4.3.3.2 Once the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment is isolated, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment has no more responsibility.

¶3189.content: - 4.4.4.1.1 When the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment is not powered, the equipment is in the No Power mode.

¶3190.content: - 4.4.4.1.1.1 Note:  in  order  to  ensure  cold  movement  detection  function,  some  parts  of  the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment may be fed by an auxiliary power supply.

¶3191.content: - 4.4.4.1.2 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall permanently command the emergency brake.

¶3192.content: - 4.4.4.1.3 Note: for the list  of  main  functions  related  to this mode, refer to chapter  4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3194.content: - 4.4.4.2.1 Used in all levels: Level 0, level 1, level 2, level 3 and level NTC.

¶3196.content: - 4.4.4.3.1 The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  has  no  responsibility  in  this  mode,  except commanding the emergency brake and (optionally) monitoring cold movements.

¶3197.content: - 4.4.4.3.2 The notion of responsibility of the driver is not relevant for the No Power mode.

¶3198.content: - 4.4.4.3.3 If it is required to move a loco in NP mode as a wagon, ETCS brake command must be overridden by external means.

¶3201.content: - 4.4.5.1.1 The  ERTMS/ETCS on-board  equipment  shall  switch  to  the  System  Failure  mode  in case of a fault, which affects safety.

¶3202.content: - 4.4.5.1.2 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall permanently command the Emergency Brakes.

¶3203.content: - 4.4.5.1.3 Note: for the list  of  main  functions  related  to this mode, refer to chapter 4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3205.content: - 4.4.5.2.1 Used in all levels: Level 0, level 1, level 2, level 3 and level NTC.

¶3207.content: - 4.4.5.3.1 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment is responsible for commanding the Emergency Brakes.

¶3211.content: - 4.4.6.1.1 The Sleeping mode is defined to manage the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment of a slave engine that is remote controlled.

¶3212.content: - 4.4.6.1.2 The  desk(s)  of  a  sleeping  engine  must  be  closed  (since  there  is  no  driver,  no information shall be shown).

¶3213.content: - 4.4.6.1.3 As the engine is remote controlled by the leading engine, its ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall not perform any train movement supervision.

¶3214.content: - 4.4.6.1.4 The  ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall perform the Train Position function; in particular, the front/rear end of the engine (i.e., not the train) shall be used to refer to train front/rear end.

¶3215.content: - 4.4.6.1.5 Sleeping mode shall be automatically detected on-board via the train interface.

¶3216.content: - 4.4.6.1.6 If  possible,  the  train  must  not  be  stopped  due  to  a  safety  critical  fault  in  a  sleeping engine. The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment should therefore try to memorise the occurrence  of  such  fault(s),  which  should  be  handled  when  the  engine  leaves  the Sleeping  mode.  The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  should  also  try  to  send  an error information to the RBC.

¶3217.content: - 4.4.6.1.7 If  a  desk  of  the  sleeping  engine  is  opened  while  the  train  is  running  (this  is  an abnormal operation), the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall switch to Stand-By mode.

¶3218.content: - 4.4.6.1.8 If  the  'sleeping  input  signal'  is  lost  (no  more  detection  of  the  remote  control),  the switch to Stand-By mode shall be made only if the train is at standstill.

¶3220.content: - 4.4.6.1.10  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall open a communication session with the RBC when at least one of the following events occurs:

¶3221.content: - in all levels, on receipt of the order to contact the RBC.

¶3222.content: - In level 2/3, when entering or exiting Sleeping mode (to report the change of mode to the RBC).

¶3223.content: -  In  level  2/3,  when  a  safety  critical  fault  of  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment occurs (to report the fault to the RBC).

¶3224.content: - 4.4.6.1.11  Note: for the list of main functions related to this mode, refer to chapter 4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3225.content: - 4.4.6.1.12  In case of balise group message consistency error (refer to 3.16.2.4.4 and 3.16.2.5.1), the ERTMS/ETCS onboard equipment shall not command the service brake.

¶3226.content: # ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

¶3227.content: - 4.4.6.1.13  When in levels 2 or 3, if no compatible version has been established between the onboard  equipment  in  Sleeping  mode  and  the  RBC,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  onboard equipment shall react as specified in 3.5.3.7 d) 2 nd  bullet but no driver's indication shall be given.

¶3229.content: - 4.4.6.2.1 Used in all levels: Level 0, level 1, level 2, level 3 and level NTC.

¶3231.content: - 4.4.6.3.1 The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  of  an  engine  in  Sleeping  mode  has  no responsibility for the train protection.

¶3232.content: - 4.4.6.3.2 The notion of responsibility of the driver is not relevant for the Sleeping mode.

¶3233.content: - 4.4.6.3.2.1 Note : The leading engine is responsible for the movement of the train. It is then the ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  of  the  leading  engine  that  is  fully/partially/not responsible for the train protection, with respect to its mode.

¶3236.content: - 4.4.7.1.1 The Stand-By mode is a default mode and cannot be selected by the driver.

¶3237.content: - 4.4.7.1.2 It is in the Stand-By mode that the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment awakes.

¶3238.content: - 4.4.7.1.3 Data  for  mission  are  collected  in  Stand-By  (see  SRS-chapter  5:  'Start  of  Mission' procedure).

¶3239.content: - 4.4.7.1.4 In Stand-By mode, the desk of the engine can be open or closed. No interaction with the  driver  shall  be  possible  as  long  as  the  desk  is  closed,  except  isolation  of  the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment.

¶3240.content: - 4.4.7.1.5 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall perform the Standstill Supervision.

¶3241.content: - 4.4.7.1.6 Note: for the list  of  main  functions  related  to this mode, refer to chapter 4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3243.content: - 4.4.7.2.1 Used in all levels: Level 0, level 1, level 2, level 3 and level NTC.

¶3245.content: - 4.4.7.3.1 The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  is  responsible  for  maintaining  the  train  at standstill.

¶3246.content: - 4.4.7.3.2 The driver has no responsibility for train movements.

¶3249.content: - 4.4.8.1.1 The  purpose  of  the  Shunting  mode  is  to  enable  shunting  movements.  In  Shunting mode,  The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  supervise  the  train  movements against:

¶3250.content: - a ceiling speed: the shunting mode speed limit

¶3251.content: - a list of expected balise groups (if such list was sent by the trackside equipment). The  train  shall  be  tripped  if  a  balise  group,  not  contained  in  the  list,  is  passed (When an empty list is sent, no balise group can be passed. When no list is sent, all balise groups can be passed)

¶3252.content: - 'stop  if  in  shunting  mode'  information.  The  train  is  tripped  if  such  information  is received from balise groups

¶3253.content: - 4.4.8.1.2 The Shunting mode shall not require Train Data.

¶3254.content: - 4.4.8.1.3 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall perform the Train Position function

¶3256.content: - 4.4.8.1.5 When  in  Shunting  mode,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  shall  not  manage  level transitions. However,  an  immediate  level  transition  order  or  a conditional level transition order shall be stored and evaluated only when another mode than Shunting or Passive  Shunting has  been  entered  (i.e.  when  the  Shunting  movement  is terminated).

¶3257.content: - 4.4.8.1.5.1 When receiving a communication session establishment order, the ERTMS/ETCS onboard in Shunting mode shall not establish the communication session, but shall store the RBC ID/phone number.

¶3258.content: - 4.4.8.1.5.2 When  in  Shunting  mode,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  shall  not  manage  RBC-RBC hand-over, except for storing the RBC ID/phone number given at the RBC/RBC border.

¶3259.content: - 4.4.8.1.6 Shunting  mode  can  be  selected  by  the  driver,  only  accepted  when  the  train  is  at standstill, or ordered by the trackside.

¶3260.content: - 4.4.8.1.7 In case of selection of Shunting mode by the driver:

¶3261.content: -  in  level  1  operations,  the  switch  to  shunting  is  always  accepted  by  the  on-board equipment

¶3262.content: -  in  level  2  and  3  areas,  the  on-board  asks  the  trackside  for  an  authorisation.  The switch to shunting is possible only after receiving such authorisation. The trackside can send a list of balises, that the train is allowed to pass while in SH, together with the authorisation

¶3263.content: # ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

¶3264.content: - 4.4.8.1.8 In case of order to switch to Shunting mode from trackside, the order:

¶3265.content: -  in level 1 is given by a balise group. A list of balises, that the train is allowed to pass after the entry in Shunting, can be sent together with the order

¶3266.content: -  in level 2 and 3 is sent via radio. A list of balises, that the train is allowed to pass after the entry in Shunting, can be sent together with the order

¶3267.content: - 4.4.8.1.9 When  the  switch  to  shunting  is  ordered  by  trackside,  a  driver  acknowledgement  is requested.

¶3268.content: - 4.4.8.1.9.1 Note: in Shunting mode the train is only partially supervised, therefore it is necessary that the driver takes the responsibility.

¶3269.content: - 4.4.8.1.10  The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  display  the  train  speed  and,  only  on driver request, the permitted speed. The display of the permitted speed shall also be stopped on driver request.

¶3271.content: - 4.4.8.1.12  Note: for the list of main functions related to this mode, refer to chapter 4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3275.content: - 4.4.8.3.1 The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  is  responsible  for  the  supervision  of  the shunting mode speed limit, and that the engine with the active antenna is tripped when passing  the  defined  border  of  the  shunting  area  (only  if  there  is  a  defined  border: balise  group  not  in  the  list  given  by  trackside,  or  balise  group  giving  the  information 'stop if in shunting').

¶3277.content: - Remaining inside the shunting area defined by a procedure or an external system outside ERTMS/ETCS (also when the shunting area is protected by balises)

¶3281.content: - 4.4.9.1.1 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall be in the Full Supervision mode when all train  and  track  data,  which  is  required  for  a  complete  supervision  of  the  train,  is available on board.

¶3282.content: - 4.4.9.1.2 Full supervision cannot be selected by the driver, but is entered automatically when all necessary conditions are fulfilled.

¶3283.content: - 4.4.9.1.3 To  be  in  Full  Supervision  mode,  SSP  and  gradient  are  not  required  for  the  whole length of the train, but must be available at least from the FRONT END of the train.

¶3284.content: - 4.4.9.1.4 Once in Full Supervision mode, if SSP and gradient are not known for the whole length of the train, an indication 'ENTRY IN FULL SUPERVISION' shall be clearly displayed to the driver until SSP and gradient are known for the whole length of the train.

¶3285.content: - 4.4.9.1.4.1 Note: this indication may also be displayed in case the train length has been increased, see 3.18.3.8.

¶3286.content: - 4.4.9.1.5 The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  supervise  train  movements  against  a dynamic speed profile.

¶3287.content: - 4.4.9.1.6 The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  display  the  train  speed,  the  permitted speed, the target distance and the target speed to the driver (this list is not exhaustive - refer to chapter 4.7 'DMI depending on modes').

¶3288.content: - 4.4.9.1.7 Note: for the list  of  main  functions  related  to this mode, refer to chapter 4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3292.content: - 4.4.9.3.1 The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  is  fully  responsible  for  the  train  protection (except for the 2 situations described below).

¶3293.content: - 4.4.9.3.2 The  driver  is  responsible  for  respecting  the  EOA  when  approaching  an  EOA  with  a release speed.

¶3294.content: - 4.4.9.3.3 When  'ENTRY  IN  FULL  SUPERVISION'  is  displayed  to  the  driver,  the  driver  is responsible for respecting speed restrictions that apply for the part of the train that is not covered by SSP and gradient data.

¶3297.content: - 4.4.10.1.1  The Unfitted mode is used to allow train movements in either:

¶3298.content: -  Areas that are equipped neither with ERTMS/ETCS track-side equipment nor with national train control system

¶3299.content: - Areas that  are  equipped  with  ERTMS/ETCS trackside equipment and/or  national train  control  system(s),  but  operation  under  their  supervision  is  currently  not possible

¶3300.content: - 4.4.10.1.2  The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  supervise  train  movements  against  a ceiling  speed:  the  lowest  of  the  maximum  train  speed  and  the  Unfitted  mode  speed limit for unfitted area (national value).

¶3302.content: - 4.4.10.1.3  The ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall also supervise temporary speed restrictions.

¶3303.content: - 4.4.10.1.4  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall display the train speed to the driver.

¶3304.content: - 4.4.10.1.5  Note: for the list of main functions related to this mode, refer to chapter 4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3308.content: - 4.4.10.3.1  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment supervises a ceiling speed and (if available) temporary speed restrictions.

¶3309.content: - 4.4.10.3.2  The driver must respect the existing line-side signals and is fully responsible for train movements.

¶3312.content: - 4.4.11.1.1  The  Staff  Responsible  mode  allows  the  driver  to  move  the  train  under  his  own responsibility in an ERTMS/ETCS equipped area.

¶3313.content: - 4.4.11.1.2  This mode is used when the system does not know the route. For example:

¶3314.content: - After the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment starts-up (awakening of the train).

¶3315.content: - To pass a signal at danger / override an EOA.

¶3316.content: -  After a trackside failure (for example: loss of radio contact).

¶3317.content: - 4.4.11.1.3  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall supervise train movements against :

¶3318.content: - a ceiling speed: the staff responsible mode speed limit

¶3319.content: - a given distance (regarding its origin location see 4.4.11.1.3.1). The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall supervise braking curves with a target speed of zero to the end of this distance. If the train overpasses this distance (see next note) the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall trip the train

¶3320.content: - a  list  of  expected  balise  groups,  if  this  list  has  been  sent  by  the  RBC.  The  train shall  be  tripped  if  over-passing  a  balise  group  that  is  not  in  the  list.  (When  an empty list is sent, no balise group can be passed. When no list is sent, all balise groups can be passed)

¶3321.content: - balise groups giving the order 'stop if in SR'. This order shall immediately trip the train, unless the over-passed balise group is included in a list of expected balises as defined in item c)

¶3322.content: - running  in  the  direction  opposite  to  the  train  orientation  (reverse  movement protection)

¶3323.content: - 4.4.11.1.3.1 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board shall determine the start location of the SR distance as follows:

¶3324.content: - If  the  National/Default value determines the max permitted distance to run in SR mode, the starting point of this distance shall refer to the estimated position of the train  front  when  SR  mode  was  entered,  or,  already  in  Staff  Responsible  mode, when Override was activated.

¶3325.content: - If  the  max  permitted  distance  to  run  in  SR  mode  is  determined  by  the  value transmitted by the RBC, or entered by the driver, the start location of the distance shall refer to the estimated position of the train front when the distance information is received or entered.

¶3326.content: - If  the  max  permitted  distance  to  run  in  SR  mode  is  determined  by  the  value transmitted by EUROLOOP, the distance information transmitted by EUROLOOP shall be referred to one or more reference balise groups. On-board shall evaluate

¶3327.content: the distance to run in SR mode by matching the reference balise groups given with the LRBG.

¶3328.content: In case the LRBG is, due to a change of orientation, in front of the train when the distance to run in SR mode is to be determined from the EUROLOOP information, the  complete  distance  to  run  in  SR  mode  shall  be  determined  as  the  distance given by EUROLOOP plus the distance between the estimated train front end and the LRBG.

¶3329.content: - 4.4.11.1.4  Note:  Since  the  gradient  is  unknown,  the  supervision  of  the  braking  curves  in  Staff Responsible mode does not ensure that the train will not pass the given distance.

¶3330.content: - 4.4.11.1.5  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall give the possibility to the driver to modify the value of the SR mode speed limit and of the given distance. This shall be possible only at standstill.

¶3331.content: - 4.4.11.1.5.1 If a train movement is detected while the driver is entering the SR speed/distance limits, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall trigger the brake command.

¶3332.content: - 4.4.11.1.5.2 The unit, range and resolution of the SR mode speed limit and distance entered by the driver shall be as specified in A.3.11.

¶3333.content: - 4.4.11.1.6  If  the  level  is  2/3  and  a  communication  session  is  open,  the  driver  shall  have  the possibility to request a new distance to run in Staff Responsible, by selecting "Start". This triggers an MA request.

¶3334.content: - 4.4.11.1.6.1 Note: Once the SR distance is covered, the driver may have to go further.

¶3335.content: - 4.4.11.1.6.2 When entering SR mode, the value applicable for SR mode speed limit and the value applicable for  SR  distance  shall  be  the  corresponding  National/Default  values. Exception for SR distance: SR mode is authorised by RBC giving an SR distance.

¶3336.content: - 4.4.11.1.6.3 While in SR mode, the value applicable for the SR mode speed limit shall be, if available, the last value entered by the driver.

¶3337.content: - 4.4.11.1.6.4 While in SR mode, the value applicable for the SR distance shall be, if available, the last value received by the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment amongst:

¶3338.content: - the distance to run in SR entered by the driver;

¶3339.content: - the distance to run in SR given by trackside.

¶3340.content: - 4.4.11.1.6.5 When "Override" is selected, the SR mode speed limit value and the SR distance value previously entered by driver or given by trackside, if any, shall be deleted. The corresponding National/Default values shall enter in force.

¶3341.content: - 4.4.11.1.6.6 If  the  train  is  in  SR  and  receives  a  new  distance  to  run  in  SR  mode  from  the RBC,  the  stored  list  of  expected  balise  groups,  if  any,  shall  be  deleted  or  shall  be replaced by the list of expected balise groups sent together with the distance to run in SR.

¶3342.content: # ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

¶3343.content: - 4.4.11.1.6.7 If an ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment in SR mode, after having received from EUROLOOP max permitted distance to run in SR mode information, detects the main signal  balise  group  being  part  of  this  information  then  it  shall  ignore  any  new  max permitted distance to run in SR mode information from that loop.

¶3344.content: - 4.4.11.1.7  The  ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall display the train speed and the (when active)  override  (permission to pass a signal at danger, trip inhibited). The permitted speed, target distance and the target speed shall be displayed only on driver request, until the driver requests to stop their display.

¶3346.content: - 4.4.11.1.9  If  receiving  a  "track  ahead  free"  request  from  the  RBC,  the  ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment requests the driver to enter the "track ahead free" information.

¶3347.content: - 4.4.11.1.10  Note: for  the  list  of  main  functions  related  to this mode, refer to chapter 4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3352.content: - 4.4.11.3.1  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment supervises a ceiling speed, a SR distance if finite and, if available, a list of balises.

¶3353.content: - 4.4.11.3.2  The driver must check if the track is free, if points are correctly positioned, and must respect the existing line-side information (signals, speed boards etc.).

¶3354.content: - 4.4.11.3.3  When using the possibility to modify the value of the SR mode speed limit and of the given distance, the driver is responsible for entering reasonable values.

¶3357.content: - 4.4.12.1.1  The On Sight mode enables the train to enter into a track section that could be already occupied by another train, or obstructed by any kind of obstacle.

¶3358.content: - 4.4.12.1.2  On  Sight  mode  cannot  be  selected  by  the  driver,  but  shall  be  entered  automatically when commanded by trackside and all necessary conditions are fulfilled.

¶3359.content: - 4.4.12.1.3  The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  supervise  train  movements  against  a dynamic speed profile.

¶3360.content: - 4.4.12.1.4  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall display the train speed to the driver. The permitted  speed,  target  distance,  target  speed  and  release  speed  (if  any)  shall  be displayed only on driver request, until the driver requests to stop their display (this list is not exhaustive - refer to chapter 4.7 'DMI depending on modes').

¶3361.content: - 4.4.12.1.5  If  receiving  a  "track  ahead  free"  request  from  the  RBC,  the  ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment requests the driver to enter the "track ahead free" information.

¶3362.content: - 4.4.12.1.6  To be in On Sight mode, SSP and gradient are not required for the whole length of the train, but must be available at least from the FRONT END of the train.

¶3363.content: - 4.4.12.1.7  Once in On Sight mode, if SSP and gradient are not known for the whole length of the train, an indication 'ENTRY IN ON SIGHT' shall be clearly displayed to the driver until SSP and gradient are known for the whole length of the train.

¶3364.content: - 4.4.12.1.7.1 Note:  this  indication  may  also  be  displayed  in  case  the  train  length  has  been increased, see 3.18.3.8.

¶3366.content: - 4.4.12.1.9  Note: for the list of main functions related to this mode, refer to chapter 4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3370.content: - 4.4.12.3.1  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment is responsible for the supervision of the train movements.

¶3371.content: - 4.4.12.3.2  The  driver  is  responsible  for  checking  the  track  occupancy  when  moving  the  train, because the track may be occupied.

¶3375.content: - 4.4.13.1.1.1 Note: Application of emergency brakes and train trip are two different things. For example, exceeding the permitted speed leads to application of the emergency brakes, but as long as the train does not pass the EOA/LOA, it is not a train trip.

¶3376.content: - 4.4.13.1.2  The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  command  the  emergency  brakes  (no brake release is possible in Trip mode).

¶3377.content: - 4.4.13.1.3  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall indicate to the driver the reason of the train trip.

¶3378.content: - 4.4.13.1.4  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall request an acknowledgement from the driver once train is at standstill (to allow the driver to acknowledge the train trip).

¶3379.content: - 4.4.13.1.4.1 Note: This acknowledgement is mandatory to exit from Trip mode.

¶3380.content: - 4.4.13.1.5  Note: for the list of main functions related to this mode, refer to chapter 4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3381.content: - 4.4.13.1.6  Closing  the  desk  while  being  in  Trip  mode  will  not  cause  a  mode  change  but  no interaction  with  the  driver  shall  be  possible  as  long  as  the  desk  is  closed,  except isolation of the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment

¶3385.content: - 4.4.13.3.1  The  ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment is responsible for stopping the train and for maintaining the train at standstill.

¶3386.content: - 4.4.13.3.2  The driver has no responsibility for train movements.

¶3389.content: - 4.4.14.1.1  The Post Trip mode shall be entered immediately after the driver acknowledges the trip.

¶3390.content: - 4.4.14.1.2  Once  in  post  trip  mode,  the  onboard  equipment  shall  release  the  Command  of  the emergency brake.

¶3391.content: - 4.4.14.1.2.1 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall keep on indicating to the driver the reason of the train trip.

¶3392.content: - 4.4.14.1.3  The train shall only be authorised to move backwards a given distance (national value). The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  supervise  this  national  distance  for reverse  movements,  and  shall  command  the  service  brakes  if  the  distance  is  overpassed. The driver shall be informed about the reason for the brake application.

¶3393.content: - 4.4.14.1.3.1 Note:  The  ERTMS/ETCS onboard equipment performs the Reverse Movement Protection (as in PT mode, the "normally allowed movement" is backwards, then the Reverse Movement Protection avoids the train running in forward direction when in PT mode). This implies that the given distance to run backwards in PT is considered as a directional data, oriented backwards.

¶3394.content: - 4.4.14.1.3.2 After  the  release  of  a  brake  command initiated due to an overpassed distance allowed  for  moving  backwards  in  Post  Trip  mode,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board equipment shall command the service brake for any further movement in the direction opposite to the train orientation.

¶3395.content: - 4.4.14.1.4  When moving backwards in Post Trip mode, the train trip shall be inhibited.

¶3397.content: - 4.4.14.1.6  When  ERTMS/ETCS  level  is  1,  if  the  driver  selects  'Start'  the  onboard  equipment proposes Staff Responsible. When ERTMS/ETCS level is 2 or 3, the selection of Start leads  to  an  MA  Request  to  the  RBC.  It  is  the  RBC  responsibility  to  give  an  SR authorisation, or a Full Supervision MA  or an On  Sight/Shunting MA  to an ERTMS/ETCS equipment that is in Post Trip mode.

¶3399.content: - 4.4.14.1.8  Note: for the list of main functions related to this mode, refer to chapter 4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3400.content: - 4.4.14.1.9  In case of balise group message consistency error (refer to 3.16.2.4.4 and 3.16.2.5.1), the ERTMS/ETCS onboard equipment shall not command the service brake.

¶3404.content: - 4.4.14.3.1  The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  is  responsible  for  supervising  that  the  train moves  only  backwards  and  that  the  backward  movement  does  not  exceed  the maximum permitted distance (national value).

¶3405.content: - 4.4.14.3.2  The driver is responsible if moving the train backwards.

¶3408.content: - 4.4.15.1.1  The Non-Leading mode is defined to manage the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment of a slave engine that is NOT electrically coupled to the leading engine (and so, not remote controlled) but has its own driver.

¶3409.content: - 4.4.15.1.1.1 Note: This operating situation is called Tandem.

¶3410.content: - 4.4.15.1.1.2 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall use, as a necessary condition to enter in Non-Leading mode, a 'non leading input signal" from the train interface.

¶3411.content: - 4.4.15.1.1.3 If  the  'non  leading  input  signal"  is  no  longer  present,  the  switch  to  Stand-By mode shall be made only if the train is at standstill.

¶3412.content: - 4.4.15.1.2  The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  not  perform  any  train  movement supervision in Non-Leading mode.

¶3413.content: - 4.4.15.1.3  The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  perform  the  Train  Position  function;  in particular, the front/rear end of the engine (i.e., not the train) shall be used to refer to train front/rear end.

¶3414.content: - 4.4.15.1.4  When level is 2 or 3, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall report its position to the RBC, according to the previously received parameters.

¶3415.content: - 4.4.15.1.5  If possible, the train must not be stopped due to a safety critical fault in a non-leading engine. The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment should therefore try to memorise the occurrence  of  such  fault(s),  which  should  be  handled  when  the  engine  leaves  Non Leading  mode.  The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  should  also  try  to  send  an error information to the RBC.

¶3416.content: - 4.4.15.1.6  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall display the train speed to the driver.

¶3417.content: - 4.4.15.1.8  Note: for the list of main functions related to this mode, refer to chapter 4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3418.content: - 4.4.15.1.9  The supervision of linking consistency shall not be performed in Non Leading mode.

¶3419.content: - 4.4.15.1.10  In case of balise group message consistency error (refer to 3.16.2.4.4 and 3.16.2.5.1), the ERTMS/ETCS onboard equipment shall not command the service brake.

¶3421.content: - 4.4.15.2.1  Used in all levels: Level 0, level 1, level 2, level 3 and level NTC.

¶3423.content: - 4.4.15.3.1  The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  performs  NO  protection  functions,  except forwarding track conditions associated orders through DMI or train interface.

¶3424.content: - 4.4.15.3.2  The driver is responsible for obeying the orders associated to track conditions, when they are displayed by the DMI.

¶3427.content: - 4.4.17.1.1  In SN mode, according to the specific on-board implementation, the National System may access the following resources via the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment: DMI, Juridical  Recording  interface,  odometer,  train  interface  and  brakes.  This  can  be achieved through the STM interface.

¶3428.content: - 4.4.17.1.2  A limited set of data coming from balises shall be used by the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment, refer to SRS chapter 4.8 'Use of received information'.

¶3429.content: - 4.4.17.1.3  Note: for the list of main functions related to this mode, refer to chapter 4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3433.content: - 4.4.17.3.1  No train supervision functionality is provided by the ERTMS/ETCS  on-board equipment. In case the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment is interfaced to the National System  through  an  STM,  refer  to  the  FFFIS  STM  (Subset  035)  for  the  functionality provided by ERTMS/ETCS on-board.

¶3436.content: - 4.4.17.4.1  The National System is responsible for all train supervision and protection functions.

¶3437.content: - 4.4.17.4.2  The National System is responsible for issuing and revoking brake command.

¶3438.content: - 4.4.17.4.3  The  National  System  is  responsible  for  maintaining  national  system  behaviour  and interact with national trackside equipment.

¶3439.content: - 4.4.17.4.4  The National System is responsible for interaction with the driver.

¶3441.content: - 4.4.17.5.1  The responsibility of the driver depends on the National System in use.

¶3444.content: - 4.4.18.1.1  The Reversing mode allows the driver to change the direction of movement of the train and  drive  from  the  same  cab,  i.e.  the  train  orientation  remains  unchanged.  This  is possible only in areas so marked by trackside.

¶3445.content: - 4.4.18.1.2  Note: This mode is used to allow the train to escape from a dangerous situation and to reach as fast as possible a 'safer' location.

¶3446.content: - 4.4.18.1.3  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall supervise train movements against :

¶3447.content: - a ceiling speed: the Reversing mode speed limit given from trackside

¶3448.content: - a  distance  to  run  in  the  direction  opposite  to  the  train  orientation,  given  from trackside. The emergency brake shall be commanded if overpassing this distance

¶3449.content: - 4.4.18.1.4  After  the  release  of  a  brake  command  initiated  due  to  an  overpassed  reversing distance, and while the reversing distance is still overpassed, the ERTMS/ETCS onboard equipment shall command the emergency brake for any further movement in the direction opposite to the train orientation.

¶3450.content: - 4.4.18.1.5  The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  display  the  train  speed,  the  permitted speed and the remaining distance to run.

¶3451.content: - 4.4.18.1.6  In  case  the  SBI  supervision  limit  is  exceeded  (refer  to  chapter  3  table  5,  triggering condition  t4),  the  ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall command the emergency brake instead of the service brake. For the revocation of the brake command, refer to 3.13.10.2.4.

¶3452.content: - 4.4.18.1.7  The  position  reports  sent  when  in  reversing  mode  shall  refer  to  the  location  of  the driving cab (as before reversing).

¶3453.content: - 4.4.18.1.8  Note:  The  ERTMS/ETCS  onboard  equipment  performs  the  Reverse  Movement Protection (as in RV mode, the "normally allowed movement" is backwards, then the Reverse Movement Protection avoids the train running in forward direction when in RV mode).  This  implies  that  the  given  distance  to  run  in  reversing  is  considered  as  a directional data, oriented backwards.

¶3454.content: - 4.4.18.1.9  Note: for the list of main functions related to this mode, refer to chapter 4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3455.content: - 4.4.18.1.10  In case of balise group message consistency error (refer to 3.16.2.4.4 and 3.16.2.5.1), the ERTMS/ETCS onboard equipment shall not command the service brake.

¶3456.content: - 4.4.18.1.11  In case there is an alarm reporting a malfunction for the onboard balise transmission function, the ERTMS/ETCS onboard equipment shall ignore this alarm.

¶3457.content: - 4.4.18.1.12  In  case  the  ERTMS/ETCS  system  version  number  X  transmitted  by  any  balise  is greater  than  the  highest  version  X  supported  by  the  onboard  equipment  (refer  to 3.17.3.5), the information from this balise shall be ignored, the train shall not be tripped and the driver shall not be informed.

¶3461.content: - 4.4.18.3.1  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment supervises a ceiling speed and a distance to run in reverse direction.

¶3462.content: - 4.4.18.3.2  The driver must keep the train movement inside the received distance to run.

¶3465.content: - 4.4.19.1.1  The  Limited  Supervision  mode  enables  the  train  to  be  operated  in  areas  where trackside information can be supplied to realise background supervision of the train.

¶3466.content: - 4.4.19.1.2  Limited supervision can not be selected by the driver, but shall be entered automatically when commanded by trackside and all necessary conditions are fulfilled.

¶3467.content: - 4.4.19.1.3  The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  supervise  train  movements  against  a dynamic speed profile.

¶3468.content: - 4.4.19.1.4  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall display the train speed and the release speed,  if  any  (this  list  is  not  exhaustive  -  refer  to  chapter  4.7  'DMI  depending  on modes'). Upon request by trackside (refer to clauses 4.4.19.1.4.2 to 4.4.19.1.4.6) if the generic LS  function marker  is stored on-board  or if the conditions in clause 4.4.19.1.4.7 are fulfilled, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall also display the lowest speed amongst:

¶3469.content: - the lowest MRSP element between the minimum safe front end of the train and the EOA/LOA, AND

¶3470.content: - the target speed at the EOA/LOA

¶3471.content: - 4.4.19.1.4.1 The speed resulting from 4.4.19.1.4 a) and b) is called the Lowest Supervised Speed within the Movement Authority (LSSMA)

¶3472.content: - 4.4.19.1.4.2 Upon  an  order  to  toggle  on  the  LSSMA  display,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board equipment shall start a delay timer:

¶3473.content: - For order received from RBC/RIU: at the value of the time stamp of the message including the order.

¶3474.content: - For  order  received  from  balise  group:  at  the  time  of  passage  over  the  first encountered balise of the balise group giving the order.

¶3475.content: -  Exception to a) and b): for order that has been stored in the level transition buffer (see section 4.8.3): at the time the level transition is performed.

¶3476.content: - 4.4.19.1.4.3 When the delay timer value becomes greater than the time-out value given by trackside, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall display the LSSMA.

¶3477.content: - 4.4.19.1.4.4 On reception of an order to toggle (on or off) the LSSMA display, a toggle on order  which  has  not  been  executed  yet  (because  the  on-board  delay  timer  has  not reached the delay time-out value) shall be deleted by the on-board equipment.

¶3478.content: - 4.4.19.1.4.5 If  the  LSSMA  display  is  already  toggled  on,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board equipment shall toggle off the LSSMA display in case:

¶3479.content: # ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

¶3480.content: - the clause 4.4.19.1.4.3 is not immediately fulfilled upon reception of a new order to toggle on the LSSMA display

¶3481.content: - an order to toggle off the LSSMA display is received.

¶3482.content: - 4.4.19.1.4.6 When  entering  the  Limited  Supervision  mode,  the  LSSMA  display  shall  be toggled  off  by  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment,  unless  a  toggle  on  order  is received  and  leads  immediately  to  the  display  of  the  LSSMA  as  per  clauses 4.4.19.1.4.2 and 4.4.19.1.4.3.

¶3483.content: - 4.4.19.1.4.7 If the generic LS function marker is not stored on-board, the clauses 4.4.19.1.4.2 to 4.4.19.1.4.6 shall not apply and the LSSMA shall be displayed if:

¶3484.content: - the target speed at the EOA/LOA is lower than the Limited Supervision mode speed limit, AND

¶3485.content: - the LSSMA is lower than the maximum train speed.

¶3486.content: - 4.4.19.1.4.8 The generic LS function marker shall be deleted by the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment as soon as a Limited Supervision mode profile is received without it in the same balise group message.

¶3487.content: - 4.4.19.1.5  If  receiving  a  "track  ahead  free"  request  from  the  RBC,  the  ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment requests the driver to enter the "track ahead free" information.

¶3488.content: - 4.4.19.1.6  To be in Limited Supervision mode, SSP and gradient are not required for the whole length of the train, but shall be at least available from the FRONT END of the train.

¶3489.content: - 4.4.19.1.7  Note: for the list of main functions related to this mode, refer to 4.5 'Modes and  onboard functions'.

¶3493.content: - 4.4.19.3.1  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment is responsible for the background supervision of the train movement to the extent permitted by the information provided by trackside.

¶3494.content: - 4.4.19.3.1.1 Note: The Limited Supervision mode enables the train to be operated in areas equipped with lineside signals where ETCS does not have information regarding the status of some signals, i.e. not all signals are fitted with LEUs or connected to an RBC

¶3495.content: - 4.4.19.3.2  The  driver  must  always  observe  the  existing  line-side  information  (signals,  speed boards etc.) and National operating rules.

¶3496.content: - 4.4.19.3.2.1 Note: the indications  given  to  the  driver  by  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board equipment do not substitute the observance of the line-side information. In particular the  display  of  the  LSSMA, if  deemed necessary by the trackside, only complements

¶3497.content: the line-side information, e.g. in case there could be a discrepancy between this latter and the background supervision.

¶3500.content: - 4.4.20.1.1  The  Passive  Shunting  mode  is  defined  to  manage  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board equipment of a slave engine (NOT remote controlled, but mechanically coupled to the leading engine), being part of a shunting consist. This mode can also be used to carry on a shunting movement with a single engine fitted with one on-board equipment and two cabs, when the driver has to change the driving cab.

¶3501.content: - 4.4.20.1.2  The desk of a Passive Shunting engine must be closed (since there is no driver, no information shall be shown).

¶3502.content: - 4.4.20.1.3  As the engine is coupled to a leading engine, its ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall not perform any train movement supervision.

¶3503.content: - 4.4.20.1.4  The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  perform  Train  Position  function;  in particular, the front/rear end of the engine (i.e., not the train) shall be used to refer to train front/rear end.

¶3504.content: - 4.4.20.1.5  It shall only be possible to enter in Passive Shunting mode from the Shunting mode; while  in  Shunting  mode,  the  driver  shall  have  the  possibility  to  enable  the  function 'Continue Shunting on desk closure'.

¶3505.content: - 4.4.20.1.6  When the active desk is closed, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall switch to Passive Shunting mode if the function 'Continue Shunting on desk closure' is active and  the  'passive  shunting  input  signal'  is  received  from  the  train  interface.  If  the function  'Continue  Shunting  on  desk  closure'  is  not  active  or  the  'passive  shunting input  signal'  is  not  present,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  switch  to Stand-By mode instead.

¶3506.content: - 4.4.20.1.7  The special function 'Continue Shunting on desk closure' shall allow one and only one transition from Shunting mode to Passive Shunting mode. The special function shall be inactive once the Shunting mode is left.

¶3507.content: - 4.4.20.1.8  If  a  desk  of  the  Passive  Shunting  engine  is  opened  and no  'Stop Shunting on desk opening'  information  previously  received  from  balise  group  is  stored  onboard,  the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall switch to Shunting mode.

¶3508.content: - 4.4.20.1.9  If  a  desk  of  the  Passive  Shunting  engine  is  opened  and  'Stop  Shunting  on  desk opening'  information  previously  received  from  balise  group  is  stored  onboard,  the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall switch to Stand By mode.

¶3509.content: - 4.4.20.1.10  If  possible,  the  train  must  not  be  stopped  due  to  a  safety  critical  fault  in  a  Passive Shunting  engine.  The  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  should  therefore  try  to memorise the occurrence of such fault(s), which should be handled when the engine leaves the Passive Shunting mode.

¶3510.content: # ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

¶3511.content: - 4.4.20.1.11  When in Passive Shunting mode, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board shall not manage level transitions.  However,  an  immediate  level  transition  order  or  a conditional level transition order shall be stored and shall be evaluated only when another mode than Shunting or Passive Shunting has been entered (i.e. when the Shunting movement is terminated).

¶3512.content: - 4.4.20.1.12  When receiving a communication session establishment order, the ERTMS/ETCS onboard  in  Passive  Shunting  mode  shall  not  establish  the  communication  session,  but shall store the RBC ID/phone number information.

¶3513.content: - 4.4.20.1.13  When in Passive Shunting mode, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board shall not manage RBCRBC hand-over, except for storing the RBC ID/phone number information given at the RBC/RBC border.

¶3514.content: - 4.4.20.1.14  Note: for  the  list  of  main  functions  related  to this mode, refer to chapter  4.5 'Modes and on-board functions'.

¶3515.content: - 4.4.20.1.15  In case of balise group message consistency error (refer to 3.16.2.4.4 and 3.16.2.5.1), the ERTMS/ETCS onboard equipment shall not command the service brake.

¶3517.content: - 4.4.20.2.1  Used in all levels: Level 0, level 1, level 2, level 3 and level NTC

¶3519.content: - 4.4.20.3.1  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment of an engine in Passive Shunting mode has no responsibility for the train protection.

¶3520.content: - 4.4.20.3.2  The notion of responsibility of the driver is not relevant for the Passive Shunting mode.

¶3521.content: - 4.4.20.3.3  Note: The leading engine is responsible for the movement of the train. It is then the ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  of  the  leading  engine  that  is  fully/partially/not responsible for the train protection, with respect to its mode.

¶3524.content: - 4.5.1.1 The following table specifies in which modes the on-board functions are active or not. The functions are described in the 'Related SRS §' (second column of the table).

¶3525.content: - 4.5.1.2 Note: Modes are not the only thing that can influence an onboard function. This is why this  Table  is  not  enough  in  itself  to  understand  all  the  ERTMS/ETCS  onboard behaviour.  It  must  be  understood  as  a  complement  to  all  other  SRS  chapters (especially §4.7, 4.8, 4.9 and 4.10).

¶3526.content: - 4.5.1.3 Note: for DMI depending on modes, refer to §4.7.

¶3529.content: Empty case = function shall be inactive

¶3530.content: O = Optional (function is not required for interoperability, but is not forbidden)

¶3544.content: - 4.6.1.1 The indication ' 4> ' means: The condition n°4 must be fulfilled to trigger the transition

¶3545.content: - 4.6.1.2 From the mode located in the column

¶3546.content: - 4.6.1.3 To the mode that is indicated by the arrow ' > '.

¶3547.content: - 4.6.1.4 Each transition from a given mode receives a priority order (indicated by ' -px', x is the priority order) to avoid a conflict between the different transitions when they occur at the same time (i.e. in the same clock cycle). P1 has a higher priority than P2.

¶3548.content: - 4.6.1.5 Some transitions have received the same priority order. This has been decided when it is obvious that these transitions cannot occur at the same time, and so can never lead to a conflicting situation (for example, the RBC cannot give in the same time a MA for FS and a MA for OS to a given engine, this is why the transition 'from SR to FS' and the transition 'from SR to OS' have the same priority order).
